para_10: You grew up hearing money is the root of evil. Rich people are greedy. Wanting money is selfish. You absorbed these messages. Now you feel guilty wanting more. You don't ask for raises. You turn down opportunities. You spend everything because holding onto it feels wrong. If money is bad, then having money makes you bad too. Your subconscious won't let you keep something you believe is morally wrong. In the US, there's mixed messaging about wealth. Success is celebrated, but wanting money is seen as shallow. This creates internal conflict. You want money but feel bad for wanting it. So you sabotage yourself. All because your subconscious protects you from becoming what you were taught to fear. Letting go of the idea that money is bad is key. Money is neutral. It's a tool. Having money means you can take care of yourself, help others, and live without constant stress. That's not evil. That's freedom.
